[Show in full screen](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#/map/0 "Show in full screen")[Wikimedia](https://foundation.wikimedia.org/wiki/Maps_Terms_of_Use) \| © [OpenStreetMap](https://www.openstreetmap.org/copyright) | University of Lagos The **University of Lagos** ( **UNILAG**)[\[2\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-2) is a [public](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Public_university "Public university") [research university](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Research_university "Research university") located in [Lagos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagos "Lagos"), [Nigeria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria"), which was founded in 1962. UNILAG is one of the [first generation universities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Education_in_Nigeria#First_generation_universities "Education in Nigeria") in [Nigeria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria") and is ranked among the top universities in Africa[\[3\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-3) in major education publications. The university presently has three [campuses](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Campus "Campus") in the [mainland](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagos_State_University "Lagos State University") of [Lagos](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagos_State "Lagos State"). Whereas two of its campuses are located at [Yaba](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Yaba,_Lagos "Yaba, Lagos") (the main campus in [Akoka](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Akoka "Akoka") and the recently created campus at the former school of [radiography](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Radiography "Radiography")),[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-:0-4) its college of medicine is located at Idi-Araba, [Surulere](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Surulere "Surulere").[\[4\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-:0-4) Its main campus is largely surrounded by the [Lagos Lagoon](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagos_Lagoon "Lagos Lagoon") and has 802 acres of land. As of 2024, the University of Lagos admitted around 8,500 [undergraduate](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Undergraduate "Undergraduate") students annually and then enrolled over 57,000 students.[\[5\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-5) [![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/f/f4/University_of_Lagos_%28Unilag%29.jpg/250px-University_of_Lagos_%28Unilag%29.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:University_of_Lagos_(Unilag).jpg) University of Lagos[![](https://upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/0/0d/Lagoon_front_Unilag.jpg/250px-Lagoon_front_Unilag.jpg)](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/File:Lagoon_front_Unilag.jpg) Lagoon Front, University of Lagos ## History \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=University_of_Lagos&action=edit§ion=1 "Edit section: History")\] UNILAG was founded in 1962, two years after the [independence of Nigeria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigerian_Independence "Nigerian Independence") from [Britain](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"). It was one of the first five universities created in the country, now known as " [first generation universities](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Education_in_Nigeria#First_generation_universities "Education in Nigeria")".[\[6\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-6)[\[7\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-7) [Eni Njoku](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Eni_Njoku "Eni Njoku") was appointed as the first black vice-chancellor of the university in 1962, and he remained in office till 1965 when he was replaced by [Saburi Biobaku](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Saburi_Biobaku "Saburi Biobaku"). However, due to controversy surrounding his appointment, Saburi was stabbed by [Kayode Adams](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kayode_Adams "Kayode Adams"), a student radical who believed Biobaku's appointment was unfair and ethnically motivated.[\[8\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-8) On 29 May 2012, the then [President of Nigeria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/President_of_Nigeria "President of Nigeria"), [Goodluck Jonathan](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Goodluck_Jonathan "Goodluck Jonathan"), proposed to rename the University of Lagos to Moshood Abiola University in honour of [Moshood Abiola](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Moshood_Kashimawo_Olawale_Abiola "Moshood Kashimawo Olawale Abiola"), who died in jail as a political prisoner in 1998. The proposed name change became a subject of protests from students and alumni. The proposal was consequently jettisoned as the [Nigerian federal government](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Federal_government_of_Nigeria "Federal government of Nigeria") gave in to the protests incited by the proposed name change.[\[9\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-9)[\[10\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-10)[\[11\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-11) A visitation panel, created to look into the affairs of the university between 2016 and 2020 detected cases of financial abuses from top officials and ordered the university to close accounts with [commercial banks](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Commercial_bank "Commercial bank").[\[12\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-:1-12) [Folasade Ogunsola](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Folasade_Ogunsola "Folasade Ogunsola") is the current Vice Chancellor and the first female to be such in the history of the school. She was appointed on 7 October 2022 and assumed office on 12 November 2022.[\[13\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-13)[\[14\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-14) ## Academics and research \[ With approximately 57,000 students as of 2013, the University of Lagos has one of the largest student populations of any university in the country.[\[23\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-The_University_of_Lagos-23)[\[24\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-24) The University of Lagos is one of the twenty-five federal universities which are overseen and accredited by the [National Universities Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Universities_Commission "National Universities Commission") (NUC). A recent publication of [Forbes magazine](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Forbes "Forbes") ranked the school as the third best university in Africa for [entrepreneurship](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Entrepreneurship "Entrepreneurship") after [University of Cape Town](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Cape_Town "University of Cape Town") and [Makerere University](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Makerere_University "Makerere University"), tagging University of Lagos the "startup powerhouse" college for Nigerian students.[\[25\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-25) The University has been called "The university of first choice and the nation's pride."[\[26\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-26) The university's research activity was one of the major criteria used by the [National Universities Commission](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/National_Universities_Commission "National Universities Commission") (NUC) in adjudging the university as the best university in Nigeria at the Nigerian University System Annual Merit Award (NUSAMA)[\[27\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-27) in 2008. The University of Lagos, College of Medicine is associated with [Lagos University Teaching Hospital](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lagos_University_Teaching_Hospital "Lagos University Teaching Hospital")(LUTH). On 29 June 2020, the university received [robots](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Robots "Robots"), CRZR, from platform capital as a donation to fight the spread of [COVID-19](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/COVID-19_pandemic "COVID-19 pandemic").[\[28\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-28) ### Affiliate institutions and colleges \[ [edit](https://en.wikipedia.org/w/index.php?title=University_of_Lagos&action=edit§ion=3 "Edit section: Affiliate institutions and colleges")\] **Centre for Biodiversity Conservation and Ecosystem Management (CEBCEM)** The centre, established in April 2018, focuses on [biodiversity](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Biodiversity "Biodiversity") management, conservation and monitoring of sustainable ecosystem through collaborative research.[\[29\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-:3-29) CEBCEM provides a platform for research and education for students of tertiary institutions as well as advocate for environmental awareness. The Centre for Biodiversity Conservation and [Ecosystem](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Ecosystem "Ecosystem") Management is the response of the University of Lagos to the threat of biodiversity in [Nigeria](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nigeria "Nigeria"). This response includes proposing local solutions to biodiversity conservation challenges which is facilitated through institution research grants such as [TETfund](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Tertiary_Education_Trust_Fund "Tertiary Education Trust Fund").[\[29\]](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/University_of_Lagos#cite_note-:3-29) ## Administration and
